focus: Q1: What is the exact mechanism by which pending embeddings become failed? Trace enforceRetryRetentionLimits() in retry-manager.js: which rows are parked (pending-cap vs max-age), in what order, and against which embedding_status set?

graphEvents.label: Max-age retention parks old pending/retry rows before cap enforcement
Pending-cap retention parks remaining queue rows after maxPending offset
Background retry job runs retention before embedding attempts
retention executes before processRetryQueue embedding work

label: Max-age retention parks pending/retry rows before pending-cap retention is calculated
Pending-cap retention selects remaining pending/retry rows after OFFSET maxPending ordered by queue age key and id
Background retry drain invokes retention before embedding, and getRetryQueue invokes retention again
Live failed rows are all max-age parked; current backlog would overflow default maxPending=1000 if stale defaults run again
Retention parking only applies to embedding_status IN ('pending','retry')
Live status counts: failed=7105, pending=10189, retry=50, success=9623
All live failed rows have failure_reason 'Retry retention max age exceeded'
Default cap would select 9239 current pending/retry rows after queue rank 1000

direction: Pending-cap caused the observed 7105 failed rows
reason: Live failed rows all carry the max-age failure reason; no pending-cap failure reason is present in failed rows.
